title: Dynamo: Amazon's Highly Available Key-value Store (SOSP 2007)
author: Giuseppe DeCandia et al., Amazon
note: The original paper behind leaderless replication: quorums, sloppy quorums, hinted handoff, vector clocks, and anti-entropy with Merkle trees, straight from the system that inspired Cassandra and Riak.

title: Local-first software: You own your data, in spite of the cloud
author: Martin Kleppmann, Adam Wiggins, Peter van Hardenberg, Mark McGranaghan (Ink & Switch)
note: Motivates multi-leader replication with offline clients: seven ideals for local-first apps and why CRDTs are the enabling technology, illustrated with real Ink & Switch prototypes.

title: PostgreSQL Documentation: High Availability, Load Balancing, and Replication
author: PostgreSQL Global Development Group
note: Production-grade reference for single-leader replication: WAL shipping, streaming and cascading replication, replication slots, synchronous commit options, failover, and hot standby.

title: Jepsen: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL 17.4
author: Kyle Kingsbury (Jepsen)
note: Shows how replication weakens isolation in practice: multi-AZ read replicas let primaries and secondaries disagree on transaction order, producing Long Fork anomalies that violate snapshot isolation.

title: GitHub: October 21 post-incident analysis
author: GitHub Engineering
note: A real failover gone wrong: a 43-second network partition triggered automated MySQL failover, split-brain writes on two coasts, and 24 hours of recovery prioritizing data integrity.

title: About CRDTs - Conflict-free Replicated Data Types
author: Martin Kleppmann, Annette Bieniusa, Marc Shapiro
note: Curated hub for conflict resolution in multi-leader systems: explains state-based vs operation-based CRDTs and indexes the key papers, implementations, and a glossary.

title: CRDTs: The Hard Parts (Hydra conference talk)
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: Goes beyond CRDT basics: why naive implementations have anomalies and terrible performance, with concrete algorithms for list moves, text editing, and metadata reduction.

title: MIT 6.5840 (6.824): Distributed Systems Engineering
author: MIT PDOS (Robert Morris, Frans Kaashoek et al.)
note: A full graduate course on fault tolerance, replication, and consistency, with free lecture notes, classic papers, and labs where you build a replicated key-value store yourself.
